Yes, we've got an IR(incident response) retainer with Cyvantics Security. That means they're on standby for scenarios like this - to investigate, contain, and guide us through recovery

Also, I checked. All other servers have security agents running and reporting normally. The only ones missing agents were the checkout servers - which feels a bit off

1. Disconnect the infected server -> <@U047N6LUYJX> 
2. Cut off infrastructure traffic -> <@U047N6LUYJX> 
3. Disconnect DB & freeze backup -> Application
4. Detach from Shared Resource -> <@U047N6LUYJX> 
5. Take snapshots (Disk & Memory) -> <@U047N6LHU0K> 
6. Kill suspicious process or activity -> <@U047N6LUYJX>
can we start with the above asap
I've given write access to Tanya, Hamed, Daniel, and myself
Disconnecting the checkout servers from the infra now
I'm on it. Disconnecting DB access for checkout and checking if scheduled backups exist
I'll verify if any shared storage is still attached to the checkout DB
Step 3 done, DB connection to checkout is now cut. Also confirmed that we have a daily backup job in place running at 2AM everyday
Step 1 done, Checkout servers are now fully disconnected. Moving to isolate the infra from all external traffic
Step 4 confirmed, No shared storage attached to checkout, which makes sense since checkout has its standalone storage
Step 2 completed, Infra is now fully cut off from the outside world. That means the website will remain completely down until we recover
thanks please confirm here as we go
Step 5 done, Cyvantics has completed taking snapshots of the checkout servers and database. They've now moved on to forensics. @Hamed, we're good to kill the script now
Step 6 done, The Script has been killed. @Kritika Iyer let us know and double-check if anything else is pending on our side

Yes, we must have a backup. Platform team can confirm us
Correct, we do have backup which only runs at 2AM. Latest is yesterday's. We'll lose today's orders - and today's sale had huge numbers
Isolation is complete. We're good to move toward restoration using the backup storage now

@Kritika Iyer I've received the forensics report from Cyvantics Security
forensics
this is what we can inform the customers
Aslo, I just finished combing through Shay's mailbox. There's something important here, I found two emails from what looks like our monitoring vendor - ClearTech
phished_email

Here's my observation by going over the comparison
• One is from <mailto:support@clear-tech.com|support@clear-tech.com>, which matches their legitimate domain. The other came from <mailto:updates@cleartech.com|updates@cleartech.com> - note the absence of hyphen in the domain 
• The legit one points to their HTTPS portal requiring login. But the phished one uses urgency, saying 'apply immediately', and links directly to an .exe on <http://cleartech.com>. That's the one Shay clicked 
• This explains the compromise - Shay unknowingly ran the malicious executable disguised as a patch
We will take this forward in PIR , for now website is restored
Alright, since everything's back up and stable, we can now move back to the main #online-boutique channel. No further actions left here
yes, and can we also send some communication to all our internal users not to click on the links and tag such emails as phising?
